title: overflow = error + opt-in saturating
manager: When a sized or bounded integer would overflow, the default is a loud error — not the silent wraparound that turns 255+1 into 0 and ships a security bug. If wraparound or clamping is genuinely what you want, you opt into saturating arithmetic explicitly. Safe by default, dangerous only on request — the opposite of the C tradition where overflow is silent and correctness is the programmer's unpaid job.
cs: overflow -> error by default; opt-in saturating (clamp-at-bound) mode; applies to sized ints (W6.2) and bounded ranges (W6.6).
